idea: Implement an event-driven architecture using Pub-Sub pattern for the AgentLoop to improve scalability and decouple components. Introduce a message queue system like RabbitMQ or Kafka to handle task dispatching and state updates asynchronously.
pros: Enhanced scalability, improved component decoupling, asynchronous processing of tasks, better resource utilization through worker threads.
cons: Increased complexity in managing message queues, potential latency in task processing due to async communication, additional infrastructure setup for RabbitMQ or Kafka.
testingStrategy: Test message queue integration with unit tests and end-to-end scenarios using tools like Jest and Vitest. Validate message delivery, error handling, and system responsiveness under load.
rollbackPlan: Revert changes to the original AgentLoop implementation, remove message queue dependencies, and restore previous testing strategies.
